refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a KPI requirement that must be operationalized with measurable acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target), explicit duty cycle definition, and telemetry-based verification. The refined FR clarifies that cycle time is a system-level performance outcome dependent on vehicle dynamics, operator control responsiveness (including tele-operation latency), and safety constraints that may impose timing overhead.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure p95 cycle time across ≥100 representative load→travel→dump cycles under agreed duty cycle conditions. Correlate with telemetry data (vehicle speed, acceleration, operator input latency, safety system activation events) to isolate performance bottlenecks.
sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within allocated budgets.
FR-2.2: The teleoperation system shall deliver operator commands with latency ≤ latency_budget to preserve cycle time performance.
FR-2.3: The safety services system shall enforce safety constraints without exceeding safety_timing_overhead budget.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall collect and report cycle time telemetry (start/end timestamps, sub-cycle durations, latency events) for KPI verification.

candidate_dps: DP-2.1: Optimize vehicle hydraulic/mechanical response times and operator control loop latency through hardware tuning and network optimization; monitor cycle time via telemetry.

DP-2.2: Implement a hierarchical control architecture with local vehicle controllers handling sub-cycle timing, tele-operator commanding high-level actions, and safety services gating execution; allocate timing budgets to each layer.
DP-2.3: Design a real-time telemetry pipeline that captures cycle phase transitions and latency events, enabling post-hoc analysis and adaptive cycle time optimization.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-2: Implement a hierarchical real-time control architecture with allocated timing budgets for vehicle sub-cycles (load/travel/dump), tele-operator command latency (≤ latency_budget), and safety constraint overhead (≤ safety_timing_overhead). Establish a telemetry-driven monitoring and reporting system to measure p95 cycle time and identify performance bottlenecks.

potential_sources: Vehicle hydraulic response time couples to operator command latency: slow vehicle response may mask tele-operator latency issues.
Safety services timing overhead couples to vehicle control: safety gating may introduce unpredictable delays if not carefully budgeted.
Telemetry collection overhead may introduce jitter if not decoupled from real-time control loops.
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is manageable via explicit timing budget allocation and separation of real-time control (vehicle, teleoperation) from monitoring (telemetry). Recommend time-triggered architecture with bounded latency guarantees.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: CT_target and duty cycle are formally defined and stable; Risk: Vague or changing targets will invalidate cycle time optimization.
Assumption: Network link supports tele-operator latency ≤ latency_budget; Risk: Network congestion or packet loss may violate latency SLA.

Assumption: Safety constraints can be expressed as timing budgets without compromising safety; Risk: Safety-critical events (e.g., emergency stop) may require immediate action, conflicting with cycle time optimization.
Assumption: Telemetry infrastructure has sufficient bandwidth and storage; Risk: High-frequency telemetry may saturate network or storage.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-2 to explicitly define timing budgets for each sub-cycle (load_time_budget, travel_time_budget, dump_time_budget), tele-operator latency budget (latency_budget), and safety overhead budget (safety_timing_overhead). Specify telemetry sampling rate and data retention policy. Add contingency for network jitter and safety event handling.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: System-level integration point responsible for orchestrating vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services to achieve cycle time KPI. Owns cycle time measurement, reporting, and cross-subsystem timing coordination.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits cycle time telemetry (phase transitions, sub-cycle durations, latency events, safety activations) to external monitoring/analytics systems.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives high-level cycle commands (load, travel, dump) from site manager or tele-operator via tasking interface.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives safety constraints and emergency signals from Safety Services, which may gate or interrupt cycle execution.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution coordinates network-dependent latency (tele-operator command delivery, telemetry uplink) via network link interface.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural system-level owner of cycle time KPI. Cycle time measurement, budget allocation, and cross-subsystem coordination are system-level concerns. Minimal diffusion: telemetry emission and tasking/safety gating are well-defined external interfaces.

Vehicle owns sub-cycle execution (load/travel/dump) and local timing, but does not own tele-operator latency, safety gating, or system-level KPI reporting. Assigning cycle time KPI to Vehicle would diffuse responsibility for network latency and safety overhead.

Teleoperation owns operator command latency, but does not own vehicle sub-cycle timing, safety constraints, or system-level KPI. Assigning cycle time KPI to Teleoperation would obscure vehicle performance and safety overhead.

Safety Services owns safety constraint enforcement and timing overhead, but does not own vehicle dynamics, tele-operator latency, or KPI measurement. Assigning cycle time KPI to Safety Services would conflate safety gating with performance optimization.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Cycle time KPI is a system-level performance outcome that emerges from the interaction of vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services. NGHE Solution, as the system-level integration point, is the appropriate owner. It must establish timing budgets for each subsystem, measure end-to-end cycle time via telemetry, and coordinate cross-subsystem optimization.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution may lack direct control over vehicle hydraulic response or network latency, making cycle time KPI difficult to achieve if subsystems are not properly budgeted.
Risk: Telemetry collection and reporting may introduce latency or jitter if not carefully decoupled from real-time control loops.
Risk: Safety Services may impose unpredictable timing overhead if safety constraints are not expressed as deterministic budgets.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Establish a formal timing budget allocation process at NGHE Solution level: (1) Define CT_target and duty cycle formally. (2) Allocate budgets to Vehicle (load_time_budget, travel_time_budget, dump_time_budget), Teleoperation (latency_budget), and Safety Services (safety_timing_overhead). (3) Implement telemetry pipeline to measure actual vs. budgeted times. (4) Establish feedback loop for cycle time optimization and budget rebalancing.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-2 (hierarchical real-time control with timing budgets and telemetry monitoring) directly addresses FR-2 (achieve p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target). The DP is solution-neutral (does not prescribe specific vehicle hardware or network technology) and is decomposable into sub-DPs for vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services. Coupling is manageable via explicit timing budget allocation.

diffusion_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution does not have sufficient authority to enforce timing budgets on Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, cycle time KPI may not be achievable. Recommend explicit governance model (e.g., NGHE Solution owns timing budget allocation; subsystems own budget compliance).
Risk: Telemetry pipeline may become a bottleneck if not properly architected. Recommend asynchronous telemetry collection with bounded latency impact on real-time control.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary for cycle time KPI ownership. Subsystem timing budgets (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) will be allocated and monitored at NGHE Solution level. External interfaces (Tasking, SafetyAlert, Telemetry, Network Link) are well-defined and do not diffuse responsibility.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Cycle time KPI is measurable via telemetry (start/end timestamps for load→travel→dump cycle). p95 metric requires ≥100 representative cycles under agreed duty cycle. Verification is feasible if telemetry infrastructure is properly designed.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Cycle time KPI is allocated to NGHE Solution. Sub-allocations to Vehicle (sub-cycle timing), Teleoperation (command latency), and Safety Services (constraint overhead) will be defined in next decomposition level. Allocation is clear and traceable.

recommended_revisions: Formalize CT_target, duty cycle, latency_budget, and safety_timing_overhead as design parameters with acceptance criteria.
Establish governance model for timing budget allocation and compliance monitoring at NGHE Solution level.
Design telemetry pipeline with bounded latency impact on real-time control (recommend asynchronous collection with time-triggered sampling).
Define contingency strategy for network jitter and safety event handling (e.g., adaptive cycle time adjustment, emergency stop override).

decision.justification: FR-2 (cycle time KPI) is not atomic and requires decomposition into sub-FRs for vehicle sub-cycles, tele-operator latency, safety constraint overhead, and telemetry monitoring. DP-2 is sound and well-coupled, but its realization depends on proper allocation of timing budgets to subsystems. NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary, but next level must decompose sub-FRs to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services with explicit timing budgets. Proceed to next decomposition level.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within allocated budgets (load_time_budget, travel_time_budget, dump_time_budget).
FR-2.2: The teleoperation system shall deliver operator commands with latency ≤ latency_budget under agreed network conditions.
FR-2.3: The safety services system shall enforce safety constraints with timing overhead ≤ safety_timing_overhead without compromising safety.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall collect, aggregate, and report cycle time telemetry (phase transitions, sub-cycle durations, latency events, safety activations) with bounded collection overhead.

assumptions: CT_target and duty cycle are formally defined and stable.
Network link supports tele-operator latency ≤ latency_budget under agreed conditions.
Safety constraints can be expressed as deterministic timing budgets without compromising safety.
Telemetry infrastructure has sufficient bandwidth and storage for cycle time monitoring.
Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems can be independently budgeted and monitored for timing compliance.
open_risks: Network congestion or packet loss may violate latency SLA, impacting cycle time KPI.
Safety-critical events (e.g., emergency stop) may require immediate action, conflicting with cycle time optimization.
Telemetry collection overhead may introduce jitter if not properly decoupled from real-time control.
Subsystems may not have sufficient authority or capability to meet allocated timing budgets.
Duty cycle assumptions may not reflect actual site conditions, invalidating cycle time optimization.
evidence_needed: Formal definition of CT_target, duty cycle, and site conditions.
Network latency characterization (mean, p95, p99) under agreed conditions.
Safety constraint analysis: which constraints are timing-critical vs. non-critical.
Vehicle sub-cycle timing characterization (load, travel, dump) under various load and terrain conditions.
Tele-operator latency budget and network link SLA.
Telemetry infrastructure capacity and latency impact analysis.
